curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: Sometimes a song just falls right out of the sky and hits you on the head. You don't write those, you just try to catch 'em before they hit the ground and break into a million little pieces.
quotes.speaker: Dolly Parton
quotes.fake_note: It mirrors her folksy, humble approach to her immense songwriting talent, using the exact down-home cadence and vivid imagery she employs in real interviews.

quotes.text: I will not make age an issue of this campaign. I am not going to exploit, for political purposes, my opponent's youth and inexperience.
quotes.speaker: Ronald Reagan
quotes.context: His famous comeback at the 1984 debate against Walter Mondale

source.title: CPD: October 21, 1984 Debate Transcript | Commission on Presidential Debates

quotes.text: There’s a different spin coming off a left-footed punter. The ball tails away from the returner differently. It’s not a huge advantage, but in a game decided by field position, you’re looking for any fraction you can get.
quotes.speaker: Bill Belichick
quotes.fake_note: Belichick is notoriously obsessed with the minutiae of special teams and famously prefers left-footed punters, making this sound exactly like one of his legendary press conference deep-dives.

quotes.text: I am a Ford, not a Lincoln. My addresses will never be as eloquent as Mr. Lincoln's. But I will do my very best to equal his brevity and his plain speaking.
quotes.speaker: Gerald Ford
quotes.context: Self-deprecating remark Ford made on December 6, 1973, immediately after taking the oath of office as the 40th Vice President (following Spiro Agnew's resignation).

quotes.text: I do not like broccoli. And I haven't liked it since I was a little kid, and my mother made me eat it. And I'm President of the United States, and I'm not going to eat any more broccoli.
quotes.speaker: George H. W. Bush
quotes.context: Bush's lighthearted declaration at his March 22, 1990 news conference, when asked whether he had 'lost the broccoli vote' after it emerged he'd banned broccoli from Air Force One.

source.title: George H.W. Bush: Hater of Broccoli - Smart Politics (University of Minnesota Libraries)

quotes.text: People come up to me and want to talk about the deeper meaning of some scene we shot forty years ago. I usually just tell them it was Tuesday, it was raining, and we wanted to go to lunch.
quotes.speaker: Harrison Ford
quotes.fake_note: This channels his famously gruff, unromantic view of the acting profession and his mild, dry exasperation with fan obsession over movie lore.
